hero.subtitle: A multi-agent collaboration framework built on prompt engineering. Break down complex software lifecycles and hand them over to your virtual Analyst, Architect, and Developer.

terminal.systemReady: > System: Virtual development team assembled. Awaiting tasks...

install.title: Start Building in Seconds
install.description: Out of the box. Use npx degit to directly pull the AI virtual team scaffolding into your local workspace. Say goodbye to heavy configurations.

features.subtitle: Nine core capabilities that make your AI-powered development team efficient, modular, and truly platform-agnostic.

items.title: Role Separation
items.desc: Say goodbye to chaotic single-LLM outputs. Each Agent has a precise role and focuses on what it does best.

items.title: Platform Agnostic
items.desc: Core framework based on pure text. Works perfectly with GitHub Copilot and planned support for Claude Code.

items.title: Unified Registry
items.desc: A single registry.yaml governs all AI resources, contexts, history, and toolsets.

items.title: Context Contract
items.desc: Each agent declares its prerequisites clearly to eliminate hallucinations and false inferences.

items.title: Data Tiering
items.desc: Innovative state management optimizes enormous context windows for perfectly timed memory recall.

items.title: Semantic Index
items.desc: Project specific guidelines and domain knowledge are semantically indexed and loaded on demand.

items.title: Semi-automatic workflows
items.desc: Not completely blind automation. Developers guide the design approval before code generation starts.

items.title: Modular Skills
items.desc: Dynamically mount specific skills (e.g. specialized API usage) directly to an agent.

items.title: Language Agnostic
items.desc: Whether TypeScript, Python, C#, or Rust, your AI team seamlessly adapts.

workflow.title: Seamless Development Lifecycle
workflow.subtitle: How the # directives trigger the pipeline.

items.desc1: Extract business concepts, analyze PRD documents.
items.desc2: Structured requirement breakdown and acceptance criteria.

items.desc1: Establish tech stack, plan system/module architecture.
items.desc2: Verifiable architectural blueprints and DB/API design.

items.desc1: Implement business code according to the blueprint.
items.desc2: Business domain code strictly adhering to context contracts.

items.desc1: Deep code dive, execute static and standard checks.
items.desc2: Refactoring suggestions and quality improvement reports.

items.desc1: Design test cases for core business paths.

items.name: Conductor
items.desc: The core dispatcher managing the task flow. Wakes up other agents.

items.name: Analyst
items.desc: Transforms long-form documents or messy code into solid business domain concepts.

items.name: Architect
items.desc: Based on analysis, establishes the tech stack and designs strict architectural blueprints.

items.name: Developer
items.desc: Flawlessly follows the blueprint to write elegant, production-grade code.

items.name: Reviewer
items.desc: Critiques submitted code with extremely strict standards to find flaws.

items.name: Tester
items.desc: Explores edge cases and builds automated testing scripts.

commands.title: Master Your Commands
commands.subtitle: Use simple # directives to interrupt or spawn an expert agent.

cmds.desc: Clean up AI chat history and artifacts.

cta.titlePart1: Ready to hire your AI Tech Team?
cta.subtitle: Clone the repo, open in VS Code, and type #init to start.
